1000.description_ja_long: 日本最北の離島・礼文島の最高峰で標高490m。低さに似合わず山上は森林限界を越えた別天地で、夏は約300種の高山植物が咲き競う。
1000.description_ja_short: 最北の離島・礼文島の頂。夏は高山植物が咲き競う花の山。
1000.description_en_long: The highest peak of Rebun, Japan's northernmost outlying island, at 490 m. Despite its modest height, the upper mountain lies beyond the forest limit, a world apart where some 300 kinds of alpine plants bloom in rivalry in summer.
1000.description_en_short: The top of Rebun, Japan's northernmost island, where alpine flowers bloom in profusion in summer.

1001.description_ja_long: 高松市西方に広がる溶岩台地・五色台の最高峰で標高479m。山中には崇徳上皇の御陵が残る白峰寺など四国霊場の古刹が点在する。
1001.description_ja_short: 五色台の最高峰。遍路道が通い、白峰寺など古刹が点在する。
1001.description_en_long: The highest peak of Goshikidai, a lava plateau spreading west of Takamatsu, at 479 m. Old temples of the Shikoku pilgrimage dot the mountain, among them Shiromineji, where the mausoleum of retired Emperor Sutoku remains.
1001.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Goshikidai plateau, dotted with old pilgrimage temples such as Shiromineji.

1002.description_ja_long: 対馬の中央部にそびえる標高479mの山。オオワシなどが生息する深い原生林は国の天然記念物で、八合目のお堂には清い水が湧き出る。
1002.description_ja_short: 原生林にワシ類が舞う対馬の山。森は国の天然記念物。
1002.description_en_long: A 479 m mountain rising in the center of Tsushima. Its deep primeval forest, where eagles such as Steller's sea eagle live, is designated a national natural monument, and pure water springs out beside a small hall at the eighth station.
1002.description_en_short: A Tsushima peak where eagles soar over primeval forest designated a national natural monument.

1003.description_ja_long: 能登半島の先端近く、珠洲市と輪島市の境をなす標高471mの山。最高点の黒峰は奥能登随一と謳われるブナ林に包まれ、祠が静かに佇む。
1003.description_ja_short: 奥能登随一のブナ林に抱かれた山。戦国の砦の記憶も残る。
1003.description_en_long: A 471 m mountain forming the border of Suzu and Wajima near the tip of the Noto Peninsula. Its highest point, Kuromine, is wrapped in beech forest praised as the finest in Oku-Noto, where a small shrine stands in quiet solitude.
1003.description_en_short: A mountain wrapped in Oku-Noto's finest beech forest, keeping memories of a Sengoku-period fort.

1004.description_ja_long: 鶴岡市の南に立ち、庄内平野を一望する標高471mの山。古くから信仰を集め、頂には金峯山神社が鎮座する。市民に親しまれる憩いの峰。
1004.description_ja_short: 庄内平野を見下ろす信仰の山。頂に金峯山神社が鎮座する。
1004.description_en_long: A 471 m mountain standing south of Tsuruoka, commanding a sweeping view over the Shonai Plain. Gathering worship since ancient times, it bears Kinposan Shrine on its summit and is cherished by the citizens as a familiar peak of rest.
1004.description_en_short: A mountain of faith overlooking the Shonai Plain, with Kinposan Shrine enshrined on its summit.

1005.description_ja_long: ジャングルに覆われた西表島の最高峰で標高469m。麓の古見村の背後に立つことが名の由来で、神が降り立つ山として崇められてきた。
1005.description_ja_short: 西表島の最高峰。神が降り立つ山と崇められるジャングルの峰。
1005.description_en_long: The highest peak of jungle-clad Iriomote Island at 469 m. Its name comes from the mountain standing behind the village of Komi at its foot, and it has long been revered as a mountain upon which the gods descend.
1005.description_en_short: Iriomote Island's highest peak, a jungle summit revered as a mountain where gods descend.

1006.description_ja_long: 津軽山地の南端に横たわる標高468mの山。名は文殊菩薩に因むと伝わるがアイヌ語説もある信仰の峰で、頂からは青森湾と津軽平野を一望する。
1006.description_ja_short: 津軽一円に知られた信仰の山。頂から青森湾を一望する。
1006.description_en_long: A 468 m mountain lying at the southern end of the Tsugaru Hills. A peak of faith whose name is said to derive from the bodhisattva Monju, though an Ainu origin is also proposed, it commands a sweeping view of Aomori Bay and the Tsugaru Plain from its summit.
1006.description_en_short: A mountain of faith known across Tsugaru, its summit sweeping over Aomori Bay and the plain.

1007.description_ja_long: 奈良盆地の南東にたたずむ円錐形の山で標高467m。日本最古級の神社とされる大神神社の神体そのもので、社は本殿を持たない。古代祭祀の霊地。
1007.description_ja_short: 大神神社の神体山。古代祭祀の面影を今に伝える円錐の峰。
1007.description_en_long: A conical 467 m mountain resting at the southeastern edge of the Nara Basin. The mountain is itself the sacred body of Omiwa Shrine, counted among Japan's oldest shrines, which for that reason has no main hall. It is a holy ground of ancient ritual.
1007.description_en_short: The sacred body of Omiwa Shrine, a conical peak carrying the air of ancient ritual to this day.

1009.description_ja_long: 長崎県の西方に連なる五島列島の最高峰で、標高460m。島々の連なりの中で最も高い頂として、洋上からの目印のように空へ頭をもたげる。
1009.description_ja_short: 長崎の海に連なる五島列島の最高峰。標高460mの島の頂。
1009.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Goto Islands, which stretch across the sea west of Nagasaki, at 460 m. As the loftiest summit in the chain of islands, it lifts its head toward the sky like a landmark sighted from the open water.
1009.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Goto Islands strung across the Nagasaki sea, an island summit of 460 m.

1010.description_ja_long: 沖縄本島北部、本部半島の最高峰で標高453m。山肌にカンヒザクラが植えられ、日本で最も早い桜の便りを届ける山として知られる。
1010.description_ja_short: 本部半島の最高峰。日本一早咲きの桜、カンヒザクラの名所。
1010.description_en_long: The highest peak of the Motobu Peninsula in northern Okinawa Island at 453 m. Kanhizakura cherry trees planted across its slopes make it known as the mountain that delivers Japan's earliest tidings of cherry blossoms each year.
1010.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Motobu Peninsula, famed for Japan's earliest-blooming kanhizakura cherries.

1011.description_ja_long: 隠岐・西ノ島の最高峰で標高452m。島前カルデラの中央火口丘にあたり、中腹の焼火神社は海の守り神として船人の崇敬を集めてきた。
1011.description_ja_short: 隠岐・西ノ島の頂。船人が海上安全を祈った焼火神社を抱く。
1011.description_en_long: The highest peak of Nishinoshima in the Oki Islands at 452 m. It corresponds to the central cone of the Dozen caldera, and Takuhi Shrine on its middle slopes has long gathered the reverence of seafarers as a guardian deity of the sea.
1011.description_en_short: The summit of Nishinoshima in the Oki Islands, holding Takuhi Shrine where sailors prayed for safe voyages.

1012.description_ja_long: 牡鹿半島の先端沖に浮かぶ島がまるごと一つの山をなす。全島が黄金山神社の霊地として守られ、ニホンジカが群れ遊ぶ。標高444m。
1012.description_ja_short: 島がまるごと一つの山。黄金山神社の霊地に鹿が群れ遊ぶ。
1012.description_en_long: An island floating off the tip of the Oshika Peninsula that forms one whole mountain in itself. The entire island is protected as the sacred precinct of Koganeyama Shrine, and herds of sika deer roam and play there. It rises to 444 m.
1012.description_en_short: An island that is one whole mountain, where deer roam the sacred grounds of Koganeyama Shrine.

1013.description_ja_long: 兵庫県赤穂郡に立つ標高440mの山。室町の世に播磨守護赤松氏が居城を構えた城山で、山頂には国史跡に指定された白旗城の跡が眠る。
1013.description_ja_short: 播磨守護赤松氏の居城跡を頂く城山。国史跡・白旗城の山。
1013.description_en_long: A 440 m mountain standing in Ako District, Hyogo Prefecture. In the Muromachi period the Akamatsu clan, military governors of Harima, held their castle here, and the remains of Shirahata Castle, a national historic site, rest upon the summit.
1013.description_en_short: A castle mountain crowned by the ruins of Shirahata Castle, seat of the Akamatsu clan of Harima.

1014.description_ja_long: 長崎県・五島列島の島に立つ標高439mの山。雄嶽の名を持つその頂は、海に囲まれた島の暮らしを見守るようにおおらかにそびえている。
1014.description_ja_short: 五島列島の島にそびえる雄嶽。島の暮らしを見守る頂。
1014.description_en_long: A 439 m mountain standing on an island of the Goto Islands in Nagasaki Prefecture. Its summit, bearing the name Odake, rises with easy grandeur, as if watching over the lives of the sea-encircled island.
1014.description_en_short: Odake rises on an island of the Goto Islands, a summit watching over island life.

1016.description_ja_long: 大和と河内の境に立つ双耳峰で標高437m。聖徳太子がこの山で毘沙門天を感得し「信ずべし貴ぶべき山」と名づけたと伝わる寅ゆかりの霊山。
1016.description_ja_short: 聖徳太子が毘沙門天を感得したと伝わる霊山。寅に縁深い。
1016.description_en_long: A 437 m twin-peaked mountain on the border of old Yamato and Kawachi. Legend holds that Prince Shotoku beheld the deity Bishamonten here and named it 'the mountain to be trusted and revered'; a sacred peak with deep ties to the tiger.
1016.description_en_short: A sacred mountain where Prince Shotoku is said to have beheld Bishamonten; deeply tied to the tiger.

1017.description_ja_long: しまなみ海道・大三島の中央にそびえる島の最高峰で標高436m。古くは神ノ山と呼ばれた霊峰で、麓には海山の神を祀る大山祇神社が鎮まる。
1017.description_ja_short: 大三島の最高峰。古名は神ノ山。麓に大山祇神社が鎮座する。
1017.description_en_long: At 436 m, the highest peak of the island, rising at the center of Omishima on the Shimanami Kaido. A sacred mountain once called Kaminoyama, it shelters at its foot Oyamazumi Shrine, dedicated to the gods of sea and mountain.
1017.description_en_short: The highest peak of Omishima, once called Kaminoyama, with Oyamazumi Shrine at its foot.

1018.description_ja_long: 湖南の平野にすっくと立つ円錐形の山で、近江富士の名で親しまれる。標高432m。俵藤太のムカデ退治伝説を宿す御上神社の神体山。
1018.description_ja_short: 近江富士と呼ばれる端正な円錐峰。ムカデ退治の伝説が残る。
1018.description_en_long: A 432 m conical mountain standing straight up from the plains south of Lake Biwa, affectionately known as Omi-Fuji. It is the sacred mountain of Mikami Shrine and home to the legend of Tawara Tota slaying a giant centipede.
1018.description_en_short: A shapely conical peak called Omi-Fuji, keeping the legend of the giant centipede slaying.

1020.description_ja_long: 讃岐平野の中央に円錐形の裾を引く独立峰で標高422m。讃岐富士と呼ばれてきた優美な姿は西行や虚子の句にも詠まれ、頂に薬師堂が佇む。
1020.description_ja_short: 讃岐平野に裾を広げる円錐の独立峰。讃岐富士の名で名高い。
1020.description_en_long: A 422 m freestanding peak trailing its conical skirts in the center of the Sanuki Plain. Its graceful form, long called Sanuki-Fuji, was praised in verse by Saigyo and Kyoshi, and a Yakushi hall stands quietly on the summit.
1020.description_en_short: A freestanding conical peak on the Sanuki Plain, famed under the name Sanuki-Fuji.

1022.description_ja_long: 月山・湯殿山とともに出羽三山をなす修験の山で標高414m。開祖蜂子皇子を導いた烏が名の由来と伝わり、杉木立に国宝の五重塔が立つ。
1022.description_ja_short: 出羽三山の一つで「現世の山」。杉木立に国宝五重塔が立つ。
1022.description_en_long: A 414 m mountain of Shugendo asceticism, forming the Dewa Sanzan with Gassan and Yudonosan. Its name is said to come from the crow that guided the founder Prince Hachiko, and a five-story pagoda, a National Treasure, stands among the cedars.
1022.description_en_short: One of the Dewa Sanzan, the 'mountain of the present world,' with a National Treasure pagoda among cedars.

1023.description_ja_long: 八郎潟の干拓地を西に見下ろす標高409mの山。名は栄えた修験の僧房に由来し、道には江戸の頃に村人が建てた三十三観音が並ぶ。田村麻呂伝説も残る。
1023.description_ja_short: 修験の僧房に名を由来する信仰の山。三十三観音が道に並ぶ。
1023.description_en_long: A 409 m mountain looking west over the reclaimed land of Hachirogata. Named for the monks' lodges of a once-flourishing Shugendo community, its trail is lined with thirty-three Kannon statues raised by Edo-period villagers, and legends of Tamuramaro also remain.
1023.description_en_short: A mountain of faith named for its Shugendo monks' lodges, its trail lined with thirty-three Kannon.

1024.description_ja_long: 房総半島の嶺岡山地にそびえる千葉県の最高峰。標高408mは全国の都道府県最高峰の中で最も低く、名は山頂近くの愛宕神社に由来する。
1024.description_ja_short: 千葉県の最高峰ながら、都道府県最高峰では日本一低い山。
1024.description_en_long: The highest peak of Chiba Prefecture, rising in the Mineoka Hills of the Boso Peninsula. At 408 m it is the lowest of all the prefectural high points in Japan, and its name comes from Atago Shrine near the summit.
1024.description_en_short: Chiba's highest peak, yet the lowest of all the prefectural high points in Japan.

1025.description_ja_long: 脊振山地の東端に立つ標高404mの草の山。白村江の敗戦後、大宰府を守るため築かれた古代山城・基肄城跡が眠る国の特別史跡である。
1025.description_ja_short: 大宰府防衛の古代山城・基肄城跡が残る草山。特別史跡。
1025.description_en_long: A grassy 404 m mountain at the eastern end of the Sefuri Range. It holds the ruins of Kii Castle, an ancient fortress built to defend Dazaifu after the defeat at the Battle of Baekgang, and is a national Special Historic Site.
1025.description_en_short: A grassy hill with the ruins of Kii Castle, an ancient fortress guarding Dazaifu; a Special Historic Site.

1026.description_ja_long: 本州最果て、下北半島の尻屋崎を望んでそびえる標高400mの山。一帯は寒立馬の放牧地で、低さに似合わぬ高山植物が石灰岩の頂を彩る。
1026.description_ja_short: 尻屋崎を望む最果ての山。寒立馬が草を食む放牧地が広がる。
1026.description_en_long: A 400 m mountain rising toward Cape Shiriyazaki on the Shimokita Peninsula, at the far northern tip of Honshu. The area is pasture for hardy Kandachime horses, and alpine plants unusual for such modest height adorn its limestone summit.
1026.description_en_short: A far-northern mountain overlooking Cape Shiriyazaki, with pastures where Kandachime horses graze.

1027.description_ja_long: 北海道壮瞥町、有珠山の麓の畑が昭和期の噴火で隆起して生まれた溶岩ドーム。郵便局長・三松正夫が戦時下に観測記録を残した逸話で知られる特別天然記念物。標高398m。
1027.description_ja_short: 噴火で畑が隆起して生まれた山。郵便局長の観測記録で名高い。
1027.description_en_long: A 398 m lava dome in Sobetsu, Hokkaido, born when farmland at the foot of Mt. Usu was uplifted by eruptions in the Showa era. A Special Natural Monument, it is known for the wartime observation records kept by postmaster Masao Mimatsu.
1027.description_en_short: A mountain born when eruptions uplifted farmland, famed for a postmaster's observation records.

1029.description_ja_long: 千葉県で2番目の高さを誇り、白鳥峰・熊野峰・春日峰の3峰からなる。各頂に神社を祀り、聖徳太子創建と伝わる神野寺が建つ。鹿が多く棲んだことが名の由来という。
1029.description_ja_short: 3峰からなる千葉県第2の高峰。聖徳太子ゆかりの神野寺が建つ。
1029.description_en_long: Chiba Prefecture's second-highest mountain, made up of three summits: Shiratori, Kumano and Kasuga. Each holds a shrine, and Jinyaji, a temple said to be founded by Prince Shotoku, stands here. The name reputedly comes from the many deer that once lived here.
1029.description_en_short: Chiba's second-highest mountain with three summits, home to Jinyaji Temple linked to Prince Shotoku.

1030.description_ja_long: 千葉県房総の山塊の総称で、最高点は妙見山。山頂直下の清澄寺は日蓮が出家得度した日蓮宗の大本山で、天然記念物の大杉は千年杉と呼ばれる。標高377m。
1030.description_ja_short: 日蓮が出家得度した清澄寺を頂く房総の山。千年杉で名高い。
1030.description_en_long: The collective name for a mountain mass in Boso, Chiba, topping out at 377 m on Myokenzan. Just below the summit stands Seichoji, head temple of the Nichiren sect where Nichiren took his vows; its great cedar, a Natural Monument, is called the thousand-year cedar.
1030.description_en_short: A Boso mountain crowned by Seichoji, where Nichiren took his vows; famed for its thousand-year cedar.

1031.description_ja_long: 高松市の東方、壇ノ浦を挟んで屋島と向かい合う岩峰。剣に見立てた5つの峰を連ね、宝永の地震で五ノ峰が崩れたと伝わる。中腹に八十八カ所霊場の八栗寺が建つ。
1031.description_ja_short: 剣のような5つの峰を連ねる岩峰。中腹に札所の八栗寺を抱く。
1031.description_en_long: A rocky mountain east of Takamatsu, facing Yashima across the Dan-no-ura strait. Its five peaks, likened to swords, stand in a row; the fifth is said to have collapsed in the Hoei earthquake. On its slopes stands Yakuriji, a temple of the 88-site Shikoku pilgrimage.
1031.description_en_short: A rocky mountain lining up five sword-like peaks, cradling the pilgrimage temple Yakuriji on its slopes.

1032.description_ja_long: 兵庫県姫路市の山。山上には性空上人が開いた天台宗の圓教寺が壮大な伽藍を広げ、西の比叡山と称される。映画「ラストサムライ」の撮影地にもなった。標高371m。
1032.description_ja_short: 西の比叡山と称される圓教寺の山。映画の撮影地にもなった。
1032.description_en_long: A 371 m mountain in Himeji, Hyogo Prefecture. On its top the Tendai temple Engyoji, opened by the priest Shoku, spreads its grand halls, earning the name 'Mount Hiei of the West.' It also served as a location for the film The Last Samurai.
1032.description_en_short: The mountain of Engyoji Temple, called the Mount Hiei of the West; also a film location.

1033.description_ja_long: 沖縄本島のほぼ中央、恩納村と金武町にまたがる両町村の最高峰。琉球王国の時代から文学の題材とされた名山で、北麓の海岸段丘の先には景勝地の万座毛が連なる。
1033.description_ja_short: 琉球の昔から文学に詠まれてきた沖縄本島中部の名山。
1033.description_en_long: The highest peak of Onna Village and Kin Town, straddling both near the center of Okinawa's main island. A celebrated mountain treated in literature since the Ryukyu Kingdom era, with the scenic Manzamo cliffs beyond the coastal terrace at its northern foot.
1033.description_en_short: A celebrated peak in central Okinawa, sung of in literature since the days of the Ryukyu Kingdom.

1034.description_ja_long: 男鹿半島の東部に横たわる火山で、山名は北東から吹きつける寒い季節風にちなむという。芝やカヤに覆われた頂からは八郎潟の干拓地や男鹿三山を一望する。標高355m。
1034.description_ja_short: 芝に覆われた男鹿半島の火山。名は寒い季節風に由来という。
1034.description_en_long: A 355 m volcano lying across the eastern Oga Peninsula, its name said to come from the cold seasonal wind that blows from the northeast. From the summit, covered in turf and miscanthus, the view sweeps over reclaimed Hachirogata and the three peaks of Oga.
1034.description_en_short: A grass-covered volcano on the Oga Peninsula, named, it is said, for the cold seasonal wind.

1035.description_ja_long: 房総半島に双耳峰を描く山で、滝沢馬琴の「南総里見八犬伝」の舞台として名高い。山中には伏姫が籠ったと伝わる岩屋が残り、早春の斜面をスイセンが彩る。標高349m。
1035.description_ja_short: 「南総里見八犬伝」の舞台となった双耳峰。伏姫の岩屋が残る。
1035.description_en_long: A 349 m mountain tracing twin peaks on the Boso Peninsula, famed as the setting of Takizawa Bakin's novel Nanso Satomi Hakkenden. The cave where Fusehime is said to have secluded herself remains on the mountain, and daffodils color its slopes in early spring.
1035.description_en_short: A twin-peaked setting of Nanso Satomi Hakkenden, where Fusehime's cave still remains.

1036.description_ja_long: 島根半島のほぼ中央に座し、「出雲風土記」に佐太神社の神名火山として登場する。頂には行基創建と伝わる朝日寺が建ち、宍道湖や日本海の眺めが美しい。標高344m。
1036.description_ja_short: 風土記に神名火山と記された出雲の山。頂に朝日寺が建つ。
1036.description_en_long: Seated near the center of the Shimane Peninsula, this 344 m mountain appears in the Izumo Fudoki as the sacred kannabi hill of Sada Shrine. On the summit stands Asahiji, a temple said to have been founded by Gyoki, with lovely views of Lake Shinji and the Sea of Japan.
1036.description_en_short: An Izumo mountain recorded as a sacred kannabi hill in the Fudoki, with Asahiji Temple on its summit.

1037.description_ja_long: 奈良公園の東端になだらかな芝の山腹を広げ、毎年1月の山焼きで知られる。頂に鶯塚古墳を頂き、三笠山の俗称も持つ。山上から望む奈良の夜景は新日本三大夜景の一つ。
1037.description_ja_short: 芝に覆われた奈良のシンボル。1月の山焼きで名高い。
1037.description_en_long: Spreading grassy slopes from the eastern edge of Nara Park, this hill is famed for the grass-burning each January. The Uguisuzuka burial mound crowns its top, and it is also called Mikasayama; the night view of Nara from the summit ranks among Japan's new three finest.
1037.description_en_short: Nara's grass-covered symbol, famed for the grass-burning festival every January.

1038.description_ja_long: 南房総にそびえる鋭い岩峰で、房総のマッターホルンの異名を持つ。頂上直下は岩場となり、狭い山頂からの眺めがよい。源頼朝の挙兵にまつわる伝説も残る。標高336m。
1038.description_ja_short: 鋭い岩峰から房総のマッターホルンと呼ばれる低名山。
1038.description_en_long: A sharp rock peak rising 336 m in southern Boso, nicknamed the Matterhorn of Boso. Rock faces guard the way just below the top, the narrow summit offers fine views, and a legend tied to Minamoto no Yoritomo's raising of arms also survives.
1038.description_en_short: A low but celebrated peak called the Matterhorn of Boso for its sharp rocky summit.

1039.description_ja_long: 函館市街の西端に横たわる陸繋島の山で、その山容から臥牛山とも呼ばれる。明治期に要塞となり立入禁止だった歴史が豊かな自然を残した。夜景の名所として名高い。
1039.description_ja_short: 臥牛山とも呼ばれる陸繋島の山。世界に誇る夜景で知られる。
1039.description_en_long: A mountain on a tombolo at the western edge of the Hakodate townscape, also called Gagyuzan, 'reclining ox,' for its shape. Closed off as a fortress in the Meiji era, that history preserved its rich nature. It is renowned as a spot for night views.
1039.description_en_short: A tombolo mountain also called Gagyuzan, known for a night view of world renown.

1040.description_ja_long: 岐阜市の中心にそびえるまちのシンボル。旧称は稲葉山で、頂には斎藤道三の居城を経て織田信長が天下統一の拠点とした岐阜城が立つ。植生豊かな森が保たれている。
1040.description_ja_short: 頂に岐阜城を頂く岐阜市のシンボル。信長の天下取りの拠点。
1040.description_en_long: The symbol of the city, rising at the heart of Gifu. Formerly called Inabayama, its summit bears Gifu Castle, once the seat of Saito Dosan and later Oda Nobunaga's base for unifying the realm. A forest rich in vegetation is preserved on its slopes.
1040.description_en_short: Gifu's city symbol crowned by Gifu Castle, Nobunaga's base for taking the realm.

1041.description_ja_long: 東京湾に臨む房総の山で、採石で削られた山肌が鋸の歯のように連なることが名の由来。山中には行基開創と伝わる日本寺があり、磨崖の大仏や百尺観音が岩壁に刻まれる。
1041.description_ja_short: 鋸の歯のような岩稜の山。日本寺の大仏が岩壁に刻まれる。
1041.description_en_long: A Boso mountain facing Tokyo Bay, named for quarried rock faces that line up like the teeth of a saw. On the mountain stands Nihonji, a temple said to have been opened by Gyoki, with a great Buddha and the Hyakushaku Kannon carved into its rock walls.
1041.description_en_short: A mountain of saw-tooth rock ridges, where the great Buddha of Nihonji is carved into the cliff.

1043.description_ja_long: 仙台市街の南西に円錐形の頂を突き上げる市民に身近な山。太白星すなわち金星が落ちて出来たとの伝承が名の由来で、江戸期には千石船の航海の目印にもなったという。
1043.description_ja_short: 金星が落ちて出来たと伝わる円錐峰。仙台市民に身近な山。
1043.description_en_long: A conical peak thrusting up southwest of central Sendai, a familiar presence to its citizens. Its name comes from the legend that the mountain formed where Venus fell to earth, and in the Edo period it reputedly served as a seamark for cargo ships.
1043.description_en_short: A conical peak said to have formed where Venus fell, a mountain close to the people of Sendai.

1045.description_ja_long: トカラ列島の有人島では南端にあたる宝島の山。島には英国の海賊キャプテンキッドが財宝を隠したとの言い伝えがあり、その舞台とされる鍾乳洞が残る。しま山100選。
1045.description_ja_short: 海賊の財宝伝説が残る宝島の山。しま山100選に選ばれる。
1045.description_en_long: Imakiradake rises on Takarajima, the southernmost inhabited island of the Tokara Islands. Legend has it that the English pirate Captain Kidd hid treasure on the island, and a limestone cave said to be the spot still remains. One of the 100 Island Mountains.
1045.description_en_short: A mountain on Takarajima with a pirate treasure legend, chosen as one of the 100 Island Mountains.

1046.description_ja_long: 徳島市街のどこからも望まれる市のシンボル。眉の如くと万葉集に詠まれた優美な稜線を東西に延ばし、頂からは吉野川と市街を見渡せる。桜の名所としても知られる。
1046.description_ja_short: 万葉集に眉の如くと詠まれた徳島市のシンボル。桜の名所。
1046.description_en_long: Bizan is the symbol of Tokushima, visible from anywhere in the city. Its graceful ridgeline, likened to an eyebrow in the Man'yoshu, stretches east to west, and the summit overlooks the Yoshino River and the townscape. It is also a noted cherry blossom spot.
1046.description_en_short: The symbol of Tokushima, likened to an eyebrow in the Man'yoshu, and a noted cherry blossom spot.

1047.description_ja_long: 福島市街の北部に浮かぶ市のシンボルで、古くは岑越山と呼ばれた。熊野・羽黒・羽山の三峰に出羽三山を勧請した信仰の山で、春は花見客で賑わう。標高275m。
1047.description_ja_short: 三峰に出羽三山を祀る福島市のシンボル。花見で賑わう。
1047.description_en_long: Shinobuyama floats over the north of Fukushima City as its symbol, once known as Minekoshiyama. A mountain of faith whose three peaks of Kumano, Haguro, and Hayama enshrine the Dewa Sanzan deities, it bustles with blossom viewers in spring. It stands 275 m.
1047.description_en_short: Fukushima City's symbol, enshrining the Dewa Sanzan on three peaks, lively with blossom viewers.

1048.description_ja_long: 高岡市と氷見市にまたがる二上丘陵の主峰。東峰と西隣の城山を二柱の神に見立てて二神山と呼んだことが名の由来という説があり、頂からは立山連峰や富山湾を望む。
1048.description_ja_short: 二柱の神に見立てられたと伝わる山。立山連峰の眺めがよい。
1048.description_en_long: Futagamiyama is the main peak of the Futagami hills straddling Takaoka and Himi. One theory says the name arose from likening the east peak and neighboring Shiroyama to two deities, and the summit looks out on the Tateyama Range and Toyama Bay.
1048.description_en_short: A mountain said to be named for a likeness to two deities, with fine views of the Tateyama Range.

1049.description_ja_long: 山形県鶴岡市にあり、麓にラムサール条約登録の上池・下池をたたえる緑豊かな山。東稜には戦国期に尾浦城が築かれた。頂の展望台から庄内平野や鳥海山、月山を望む。
1049.description_ja_short: 麓にラムサール条約の池をもつ鶴岡の山。戦国の城跡が残る。
1049.description_en_long: Takadateyama is a lush green mountain in Tsuruoka, Yamagata, holding the Ramsar-listed Kamiike and Shimoike ponds at its foot. Oura Castle was built on its east ridge in the Sengoku period, and the summit deck overlooks the Shonai Plain, Mt. Chokai, and Gassan.
1049.description_en_short: A Tsuruoka mountain with Ramsar-listed ponds at its foot and a Sengoku-era castle site remaining.

1050.description_ja_long: 三浦半島の最高峰ながら標高241mの低山。周囲に高い山がなく、頂からは相模湾と東京湾、富士山まで見渡せる大観で知られ、司馬遼太郎も紀行文でその眺めに触れた。
1050.description_ja_short: 三浦半島の最高峰。海と富士を見渡す大観で知られる低山。
1050.description_en_long: Ogusuyama, the highest point of the Miura Peninsula, is nonetheless a low mountain of 241 m. With no higher peaks nearby, its summit commands a sweeping view over Sagami Bay, Tokyo Bay, and even Mt. Fuji, a panorama the writer Ryotaro Shiba noted in a travel essay.
1050.description_en_short: The highest peak of the Miura Peninsula, a low mountain famed for sweeping views of the sea and Mt. Fuji.

1051.description_ja_long: 鹿児島県の奄美諸島に浮かぶ沖永良部島の山で、標高は240m。「おおやま」の名で呼ばれ、海に囲まれた島の起伏の中にその頂をもたげている。
1051.description_ja_short: 鹿児島県の奄美諸島、沖永良部島に位置する標高240mの山。
1051.description_en_long: Oyama is a mountain on Okinoerabujima, an island of the Amami group in Kagoshima Prefecture, standing 240 m high. Known by the reading Oyama, it lifts its summit amid the rolling terrain of this island surrounded by the sea.
1051.description_en_short: A 240 m mountain on Okinoerabujima in the Amami Islands of Kagoshima Prefecture.

1052.description_ja_long: 鹿児島県の種子島にある標高238mの山。山中に天女ヶ倉神社が鎮座し、天女隠れの岩と呼ばれる岩屋が残る。山名はこの岩屋にちなんでつけられたと伝えられている。
1052.description_ja_short: 山中の岩屋、天女隠れの岩が名の由来と伝わる種子島の山。
1052.description_en_long: Amamegakura is a 238 m mountain on Tanegashima in Kagoshima Prefecture. Amamegakura Shrine sits on its slopes, and a rock cave known as the Hiding Rock of the Celestial Maiden remains, from which the mountain's name is said to derive.
1052.description_en_short: A Tanegashima mountain said to take its name from a rock cave, the Hiding Rock of the Celestial Maiden.

1053.description_ja_long: 宮城県涌谷町の中央に立つ信仰の山。坂上田村麻呂が平和を祈って矢竹を立てた故事が名の由来と伝わり、頂の箟峯寺は奥州三観音の一つに数えられる。標高236m。
1053.description_ja_short: 奥州三観音の箟峯寺を頂く信仰の山。田村麻呂伝説が残る。
1053.description_en_long: Nonodakeyama is a mountain of faith standing at the center of Wakuya, Miyagi. Its name is said to come from the tale that Sakanoue no Tamuramaro planted arrow bamboo here praying for peace, and Konpoji on the summit is one of the Three Kannon of Oshu. It stands 236 m.
1053.description_en_short: A mountain of faith crowned by Konpoji, one of the Three Kannon of Oshu, with a Tamuramaro legend.

1054.description_ja_long: 日本最西端の島、与那国島にそびえる山。三角錐の姿から与那国富士と呼ばれ、頂からは年に数回、海の彼方に台湾の島影を望むことがあるという。標高231m。
1054.description_ja_short: 与那国富士と呼ばれる最西端の島の山。台湾を望む日もある。
1054.description_en_long: Urabudake rises on Yonagunijima, Japan's westernmost island. Its triangular pyramid form has earned it the name Yonaguni-Fuji, and a few times a year, the summit is said to offer a glimpse of Taiwan far across the sea. It stands 231 m high.
1054.description_en_short: Called Yonaguni-Fuji, on Japan's westernmost island; on rare days Taiwan shows across the sea.

1055.description_ja_long: 静岡市駿河区にそびえる標高216mの山。晩年を駿府に暮らした徳川家康が「遺骸は久能山に」と遺命し、山上に東照宮が営まれた。戦国期には山城も置かれた要地である。
1055.description_ja_short: 徳川家康の遺命により、山上に東照宮が営まれた駿河の山。
1055.description_en_long: Kunozan is a 216 m mountain rising in Suruga Ward, Shizuoka City. Tokugawa Ieyasu, who spent his last years in Sunpu, willed that his remains rest on Kunozan, and Toshogu Shrine was built on the mountaintop. A castle also stood on this key site in the Sengoku period.
1055.description_en_short: A Suruga mountain where Toshogu Shrine was built on the summit by Tokugawa Ieyasu's dying wish.

1056.description_ja_long: 奈良盆地南部に立つ大和三山の最高峰で標高199m。万葉集に「瑞山」と詠まれた秀麗な山で、麓には神武天皇ゆかりの橿原神宮が鎮座する。名は「火がうねる」の意という。
1056.description_ja_short: 大和三山の最高峰。万葉に詠まれ、麓に橿原神宮が鎮座。
1056.description_en_long: Unebiyama, the highest of the Yamato Sanzan, stands 199 m in the southern Nara Basin. A graceful mountain praised as a sacred hill in the Man'yoshu, it has Kashihara Shrine, linked to Emperor Jimmu, at its foot. The name is said to mean undulating fire.
1056.description_en_short: The highest of the Yamato Sanzan, sung in the Man'yoshu, with Kashihara Shrine at its foot.

1057.description_ja_long: 房総半島西端、東京湾へ突き出た岬にあり「房の大山」とも呼ばれる。戦時中は首都防衛の砲台が置かれた。山頂からは三浦半島や富士山、南アルプスまで一望できる。
1057.description_ja_short: 東京湾を望む房総の岬の山。頂から富士や三浦半島を一望。
1057.description_en_long: Oyama sits on a cape jutting into Tokyo Bay at the western tip of the Boso Peninsula and is also called Bo no Oyama. Gun batteries defending the capital were placed here in wartime. The summit commands the Miura Peninsula, Mt. Fuji, and even the Southern Alps.
1057.description_en_short: A Boso cape mountain over Tokyo Bay; the summit sweeps from Mt. Fuji to the Miura Peninsula.

1059.description_ja_long: 奈良県橿原市、大和三山の一つで標高152m。天から降ったと伝わる神聖な山で、持統天皇の「春過ぎて夏来たるらし」の歌をはじめ万葉集に幾度も詠まれた歌の聖地。
1059.description_ja_short: 天から降ったと伝わる大和三山の聖なる山。万葉の歌枕。
1059.description_en_long: Amanokaguyama, one of the Yamato Sanzan in Kashihara, Nara, stands 152 m. A sacred mountain said to have descended from heaven, it is a holy ground of poetry sung many times in the Man'yoshu, including Empress Jito's verse on spring passing into summer.
1059.description_en_short: A sacred Yamato Sanzan peak said to have descended from heaven, a storied site of Man'yoshu poetry.

1060.description_ja_long: 奈良県橿原市にある大和三山の一つで、最も北に位置する。余分なところがない円錐形の姿から「耳無し」に転じたという山名を持ち、麓は桜の名所として親しまれる。
1060.description_ja_short: 円錐形が美しい大和三山の一峰。名は「耳無し」に由来。
1060.description_en_long: Miminashiyama is the northernmost of the Yamato Sanzan in Kashihara, Nara. Its name is said to derive from miminashi, meaning without ears, for a conical form so clean it has nothing to spare, and its foot is cherished as a cherry blossom spot.
1060.description_en_short: A beautifully conical peak of the Yamato Sanzan; the name derives from miminashi, without ears.

1061.description_ja_long: 松島湾の対岸、宮戸島の最高峰で標高105m。頂からは湾に散らばる260余りの島々を一望でき、その眺めは「壮観」と讃えられ松島四大観の一つに数えられている。
1061.description_ja_short: 松島の島々を一望する宮戸島の頂。四大観の「壮観」。
1061.description_en_long: Otakamori is the highest point of Miyatojima, across the water from Matsushima Bay, at 105 m. The summit takes in at a glance the 260-odd islands scattered over the bay, a view praised as magnificent and counted among the Four Great Views of Matsushima.
1061.description_en_short: Miyatojima's summit overlooking the isles of Matsushima; the magnificent one of the Four Great Views.
